revision.text:
[[de:Totenfest des Osiris]][[fr:Fête Ouag]] '''Festival of Osiris''' is an ancient Egyptian festival ==Overview== {{Infobox hieroglyphs |TITLE = Osiris Wag Festival |NAME = V4-G1-W11-W3 |NAME-TRANSCRIPTION = Heb-wag-(Osiris)
''ḥb-w3g'' |NAME-EXPLANATION = ''Wag Festival (Festival of Osiris and the Dead)'' }} The '''Festival of Osiris''' (also '''Wag Festival''') was celebrated annually in {{ill|Ancient Egypt|en|Ancient Egypt|qid=Q11768|lt=Ancient Egypt|1=Ancient Egypt}} in honor of the god {{ill|Osiris|en|Osiris|qid=Q46491|lt=Osiris|1=Osiris}} on his day of death, {{ill|Season of the Inundation|en|Season of the Inundation|qid=Q770568|lt=18th of Achet|1=Season of the Inundation}} I. The {{ill|Cult of Isis and Osiris|de|Isis-Osiris-Kult|ja|イシスとオシリスの信仰|zh|伊西斯與奧西里斯信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cult of Isis and Osiris|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is first attested in writing in the {{ill|Fifth Dynasty of Egypt|en|Fifth Dynasty of Egypt|qid=Q269237|lt=Fifth Dynasty|1=Fifth Dynasty of Egypt}}. {{ill|Egyptologists|de|Ägyptologe|ja|エジプト学者|zh|埃及学家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptologists|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to エジプト学|1=Egyptology|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q145903}} date its origins to at least the period of the {{ill|Unification of Upper and Lower Egypt|de|Reichseinigung (Ägypten)|ja|上下エジプトの統一|zh|上下埃及統一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unification of Upper and Lower Egypt|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The festival was originally tied to the {{ill|Sothic calendar|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|ja|ソティス暦|zh|索提斯曆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sothic calendar|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, coinciding with the {{ill|heliacal rising|de|heliakisch|ja|ヒライアカル|zh|偕日升|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/heliacal rising|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of {{ill|Sirius|en|Sirius|qid=Q3409|lt=Sirius|1=Sirius}} and the subsequent {{ill|Flooding of the Nile|en|Flooding of the Nile|qid=Q1519552|lt=Nile flood|1=Flooding of the Nile}} shortly thereafter. In the {{ill|Egyptian calendar|en|Egyptian calendar|qid=Q254101|lt=Egyptian calendar|1=Egyptian calendar}}, the festival was thus fixed to the 18th day of Achet I. == Background == === Mythology === Osiris was also regarded as the god of the {{ill|Nile|en|Nile|qid=Q3392|lt=Nile|1=Nile}}. His death in the {{ill|Osiris myth|en|Osiris myth|qid=Q28246|lt=Osiris myth|1=Osiris myth}} symbolized the drying up of the Nile and began with the appearance of {{ill|Isis-Sopdet|de|Isis-Sopdet|ja|イシス＝ソプデト|zh|伊西斯-索普德特|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isis-Sopdet|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who determined the timing of the Nile flood. The blood of Osiris represented the Nile water flowing into the {{ill|Mediterranean Sea|en|Mediterranean Sea|qid=Q4918|lt=Mediterranean Sea|1=Mediterranean Sea}} following his murder by {{ill|Set (deity)|en|Set (deity)|qid=Q131795|lt=Set (Egyptian mythology)|1=Set (deity)}}. Mythologically, Osiris died on the day of the Nile’s lowest water level. In the {{ill|Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|en|Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|qid=Q187979|lt=Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|1=Early Dynastic Period of Egypt}}, Sirius appeared around June 5th. In the {{ill|Copts|en|Copts|qid=Q146744|lt=Coptic|1=Copts}} liturgy, this date represents the first day of the first month of Achet and the start of the year. About 18 days later, the Nile flood arrived in the Nile Delta around the 19th of Achet I during the {{ill|Solstice|en|Solstice|qid=Q123524|lt=solstice|1=Solstice}}, following the lowest water level on the 18th of Achet I. In the so-called {{ill|Book of Nut|en|Book of Nut|qid=Q2005559|lt=Book of Nut|1=Book of Nut}}, Osiris is described in his role as the sacred Nile water: {{quote|Usually {{ill|Ra|en|Ra|qid=Q1252904|lt=Ra (Egyptian mythology)|1=Ra}} enters in the evening the land of life, the {{ill|Duat|en|Duat|qid=Q871389|lt=Duat|1=Duat}}. It is beautiful in the hand of his father Osiris. This is the water. He (Ra) purifies himself in it. He cleanses himself therein, that is, the water.|Text of the Book of NutAlexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne.'' Copenhagen 2007, p. 74.}} === Festival proceedings === In the ceremonial {{ill|Procession|en|Procession|qid=Q115665045|lt=processions|1=Procession}}, Egyptians carried the vessel of Osiris as the "coffin of Osiris." The sacred Nile water, representing Osiris himself, was held within this vessel. Its lid was decorated in the shape of Osiris, protectively watching over the Nile water. The tradition of the Festival of Osiris was preserved nearly unchanged by the {{ill|Ptolemaic dynasty|en|Ptolemaic dynasty|qid=Q131976|lt=Ptolemies|1=Ptolemaic dynasty}} and was later elevated as the cult of the {{ill|Osirian Mysteries|de|Osiris-Mysterien|ja|オシリスの秘儀|zh|奧西里斯密儀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osirian Mysteries|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Literature == * Hans Förster: ''Die Anfänge von Weihnachten und Epiphanias. Eine Anfrage an die Entstehungshypothesen'' (= ''Studien und Texte zu Antike und Christentum.'' Volume 46). Mohr Siebeck, Tübingen 2007, ISBN 978-3-16-149399-7. * {{ill|Kathrin Kleibl|de|Kathrin Kleibl|ja|カトリン・クライブ|zh|卡特琳·克萊布爾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kathrin Kleibl|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Die Wasserkrypten in den hellenistischen und römischen Heiligtümern der ägyptischen Götter im Mittelmeerraum.'' Hamburg 2003 (Magisterarbeit, Universität Hamburg, 2003), [http://archiv.ub.uni-heidelberg.de/propylaeumdok/volltexte/2007/37/ online (PDF; 6.9 MB)]. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|zh|羅爾夫·克勞斯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Sothis- und Monddaten. Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens.'' Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Incorrect ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}} * {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|en|Richard Anthony Parker|qid=Q2148992|lt=Richard A. Parker|1=Richard Anthony Parker}}: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt.'' University of Chicago Press, Chicago (IL) 1950. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|西格弗里德·肖特|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135491464|1=Siegfried Schott}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten.'' Mainz 1950. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|ja|アレクサンドラ・フォン・リーフェン|zh|亞歷山德拉·馮·利芬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne.'' Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. == References == ==References== {{translated page|de|Totenfest des Osiris|version=241304742|comment=Imported full de history}} [[ja:オシリスのワグ祭]] [[de:Totenfest des Osiris]] [[fr:F%C3%AAte Ouag]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} [[fr:Fête Ouag]] {{wikidata link|Q3092806}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian funerary practices]] [[Category:Festival Calendar of the Month Achet 1]] [[Category:Osiris]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]
